title: Introduction
description: DexCode is an AI-first slide authoring and refinement tool for developers who never want to leave their terminal.
content: What is DexCode? DexCode is an open-source slide authoring tool designed for developers. Instead of switching to PowerPoint or Google Slides, you create and refine presentations directly from your terminal using AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, or Cursor. Slides are written as MDX files — Markdown with React components — and rendered in real-time with hot module replacement. Every change you make (or your AI agent makes) is reflected instantly in the browser. Key Concepts - AI-First: Your coding agent generates and edits slide files. You prompt, it builds. - Per-Slide Parallel Editing: After the initial draft, assign one agent per slide for simultaneous refinements. - Live Preview: Hot reload keeps your browser preview in sync as files change. - Shareable: Use Cloudflare Tunnel to share a live URL with your team, or export to PDF/PPTX. Who is DexCode For? DexCode is built for: - Developers who create sprint reviews, technical presentations, or pitch decks - Teams that use AI coding agents in their daily workflow - Anyone who wants to stay in their terminal and avoid context switching How It Works 1. Clone the DexCode repo and run npm install && npm run dev 2. Open your AI agent (e.g., claude) and prompt it to create slides 3. The agent generates MDX files; slides appear in the browser instantly 4. Refine slides with follow-up prompts — in parallel if you want 5. Share via Cloudflare Tunnel, export to PDF, or present directly

title: Installation
description: Clone the DexCode repository and install dependencies to start creating slides.
content: Prerequisites - Node.js 18 or later - npm (included with Node.js) - An AI coding agent (Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, etc.) Install DexCode Clone the repository Install dependencies Start the development server Your slides will be available at http://localhost:3000. Verify Installation Open your browser and navigate to http://localhost:3000. You should see the default DexCode slide deck with sample slides. DexCode uses Vite's HMR (Hot Module Replacement). Any changes to MDX slide files are reflected instantly in the browser — no manual refresh needed. Project Structure - slides/ — Your MDX slide files live here. Each file is one slide. - deck.config.ts — Deck-level configuration (title, theme, author). - components/ — Built-in and custom React components for slides.

title: Quick Start
description: Create your first slide deck with DexCode and an AI agent in under 5 minutes.
content: Create Your First Deck Once DexCode is installed and the dev server is running, open your AI coding agent and start prompting. Using Claude Code Claude Code will generate MDX files in the slides/ directory. As each file is saved, the browser preview updates instantly. Using Other Agents DexCode works with any agent that can edit files: Open the DexCode project in Cursor and use the AI chat to generate slides. Refine Your Slides After the initial draft, refine individual slides with follow-up prompts: Parallel Editing For faster iteration, edit multiple slides simultaneously: The agent will spawn subagents to edit each slide concurrently. Present & Share Presenter Mode — Press P in the browser to open presenter mode with a separate fullscreen window. Export to PDF — Run npm run export:pdf to generate a high-quality PDF via Playwright. Share via Tunnel — Ask your agent to expose the deck via Cloudflare Tunnel for real-time sharing. DexCode decks are live web pages. Anyone with the URL can view the latest version in real time.

title: Creating Decks
description: Learn how to create slide decks from scratch using AI agents and DexCode's MDX format.
content: Deck Structure Every DexCode deck is a directory containing MDX slide files and a configuration file: The deck.config.ts File The deck configuration defines your deck's metadata and theme: Writing Slides in MDX Each slide is an MDX file that combines Markdown with React components: AI-Assisted Creation The most efficient way to create decks is through AI agents: Single-Prompt Generation Context-Aware Generation Point the agent at your project for context-aware slides: The more context you give the agent, the better the output. Reference specific files, directories, or git history for accurate content. Slide Layouts DexCode includes 100+ built-in slide templates. Specify a layout in the frontmatter: Available layouts include default, cover, two-column, three-column, image-left, image-right, centered, dashboard, and many more.

title: Parallel Editing
description: Edit multiple slides simultaneously using AI agent subprocesses for faster iteration.
content: What is Parallel Editing? Parallel editing is DexCode's core differentiator. Instead of revising slides one at a time, you can assign edits to multiple slides simultaneously. Your AI agent spawns subagents — one per slide — that work concurrently. How It Works When you prompt an agent like Claude Code with a batch edit instruction, it: 1. Identifies which slides need changes 2. Spawns a subagent for each target slide 3. Each subagent edits its assigned MDX file independently 4. Hot reload updates the browser preview as each file is saved Benefits | Traditional Workflow | Parallel Editing | |---|---| | Edit slide 3, wait, review | All 3 slides edited simultaneously | | Edit slide 5, wait, review | Review all changes at once | | Edit slide 8, wait, review | Single review cycle | | ~15 minutes | ~5 minutes | Best Practices Be Specific Give clear, actionable instructions for each slide: Batch Related Changes Group related edits into a single parallel request: Use Micro-Tuning For final polish, use AI micro-tuning on individual elements: Parallel editing works best with agents that support subagent spawning, such as Claude Code's Agent tool.

title: Sharing & Presenting
description: Share your DexCode slides via Cloudflare Tunnel, export to PDF/PPTX, or present directly.
content: Sharing Options DexCode provides multiple ways to share your slides with others. Cloudflare Tunnel The fastest way to share a live deck. Ask your AI agent: Or run the tunnel manually: This generates a public URL (e.g., https://abc123.trycloudflare.com) that anyone can access. The viewer sees your slides in real time — any updates you make are reflected immediately. Cloudflare Tunnel URLs are publicly accessible. Do not share confidential content without additional access controls. PDF Export Generate a high-quality PDF of your deck: This uses Playwright to render each slide at 1920x1080 and produce a print-ready PDF. The output is saved to exports/deck.pdf. PPTX Export Export to PowerPoint format for offline sharing: PPTX export converts each slide to an image-based PowerPoint slide. Interactive components (charts, animations) are rendered as static images. Presenter Mode Press P in the browser or click the presenter mode button to open a two-window presentation setup: - Presenter window: Shows current slide, next slide preview, speaker notes, and timer - Audience window: Shows the current slide fullscreen (ideal for projector output) Both windows stay synchronized via BroadcastChannel API — no server needed. Keyboard Shortcuts | Key | Action | |---|---| | → or Space | Next slide | | ← | Previous slide | | P | Toggle presenter mode | | F | Toggle fullscreen | | Esc | Exit fullscreen/presenter | Embedding Since DexCode decks are standard web pages, you can embed them in other sites using an iframe:

title: Theme Customization
description: Customize the look and feel of your DexCode slides with themes, colors, and typography.
content: Theme Configuration Every deck has a deck.config.ts file that controls its visual appearance: Color Presets DexCode includes several built-in color presets: | Preset | Primary | Background | Style | |---|---|---|---| | default | #e17120 | #111111 | Dark with orange accent | | ocean | #3b82f6 | #0a1628 | Dark navy with blue | | forest | #22c55e | #0a1a0e | Dark green theme | | sunset | #f59e0b | #1a1008 | Warm amber tones | | minimal | #333333 | #ffffff | Clean light theme | Apply a preset: Custom Backgrounds Set per-slide backgrounds using frontmatter: Background Options - Solid color: background: "#1a1a2e" - Gradient: background: "linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e, #16213e)" - Image: background: "url(/images/hero.jpg)" - Image with overlay: background: "url(/images/bg.jpg)" with backgroundOverlay: "rgba(0,0,0,0.6)" Component Styling Built-in components automatically adapt to your theme colors. You can also override individual component styles: Ask your AI agent to adjust styling: "Make slide 5 use a dark gradient background with orange accent highlights."

title: Component Overview
description: DexCode includes 100+ built-in slide components for charts, layouts, code blocks, and more.
content: Built-in Components DexCode slides are MDX files — Markdown with React components. You can use any built-in component directly in your slides without importing. Available Components Data Visualization - BarChart — Vertical and horizontal bar charts - LineChart — Line charts with multiple series - PieChart — Pie and donut charts - AreaChart — Stacked and filled area charts - KpiCards — Key performance indicator cards with trend arrows Layout - Columns — Multi-column layouts (2, 3, or custom widths) - CardGrid — Grid of styled cards - ImageLeft / ImageRight — Image + text split layouts - CenteredHeading — Full-bleed centered heading slide - Dashboard — Multi-widget dashboard layout Content - CodeBlock — Syntax-highlighted code with copy button - Table — Styled tables with header formatting - Timeline — Horizontal or vertical timelines - Steps — Numbered step sequences - Checklist — Interactive checklists - Blockquote — Styled quotations Visual - Icons — 1000+ Lucide icons available - Shapes — Decorative SVG shapes - BigNumber — Large stat/number display - TeamProfiles — Team member cards with avatars - BeforeAfter — Side-by-side comparison Diagrams - Flowchart — Process flow diagrams - Pyramid — Hierarchical pyramids - VennDiagram — Overlapping circles - SWOT — SWOT analysis matrix - CycleDigram — Circular process diagrams Usage Example

title: Charts
description: Use built-in chart components to visualize data in your DexCode slides.
content: Chart Components DexCode includes several chart types that automatically style themselves to match your deck theme. BarChart Display data as vertical or horizontal bars: Props ", description: "Chart data points", required: true }, { flag: "title", type: "string", description: "Chart title displayed above the chart" }, { flag: "color", type: "string", default: "theme.primary", description: "Bar color" }, { flag: "horizontal", type: "boolean", default: "false", description: "Render as horizontal bars" }, { flag: "showValues", type: "boolean", default: "true", description: "Show values on bars" }, ]} /> LineChart Display trends over time: PieChart Show proportional data: AreaChart Filled area charts for volume visualization: Tell your AI agent: "Add a bar chart showing monthly active users from January to June with the values 1200, 1500, 1800, 2100, 2500, 3000."

title: Code Blocks
description: Display syntax-highlighted code in your DexCode slides with theme-aware styling.
content: Code Blocks DexCode uses Shiki for syntax highlighting with the One Dark Pro theme. Code blocks are automatically styled to match your deck's dark theme. Basic Usage Use standard Markdown fenced code blocks with a language identifier: Supported Languages DexCode supports 100+ programming languages through Shiki, including: - TypeScript / JavaScript - Python - Rust - Go - Java - C / C++ - Ruby - Swift - SQL - Bash / Shell - HTML / CSS - JSON / YAML / TOML Multi-Language Code Show code in multiple languages using the Tabs component. Each tab contains a code block in a different language, letting viewers switch between implementations. TypeScript Example Python Example Go Example Terminal Blocks For shell commands, use the Terminal component to render a styled terminal window: Code with Explanations Pair code with descriptive text using the Columns component. Place the code block on one side and explanation on the other for a clean layout. The configuration file controls deck metadata, slide count targets, and visual theme selection. All settings can be overridden per-slide in the MDX frontmatter. Code blocks include a copy-to-clipboard button that appears on hover. This works in both preview and presenter mode.

title: Layouts
description: Use built-in layout components to structure your DexCode slides with columns, grids, and split views.
content: Layout System DexCode slides support multiple layout options through frontmatter and components. Frontmatter Layouts Set the layout in your slide's frontmatter: Available Layouts | Layout | Description | |---|---| | default | Standard single-column with padding | | cover | Full-bleed cover slide with centered content | | two-column | Equal two-column split | | three-column | Three equal columns | | image-left | Image on left, text on right | | image-right | Text on left, image on right | | centered | Vertically and horizontally centered | | dashboard | Multi-widget grid layout | | section | Section divider slide | Columns Component For custom column widths, use the Columns component: Three Columns CardGrid Display items in a responsive grid: Split Views Image + Text Before & After Ask your AI agent to suggest the best layout: "What layout would work best for showing a product screenshot with feature bullets?"

title: CLI Commands
description: Complete reference for DexCode's command-line interface.
content: Commands DexCode is managed through npm scripts defined in package.json. Development npm run dev Start the development server with hot module replacement. Build & Export npm run build Build the deck for production deployment. npm run export:pdf Export the deck to a PDF file using Playwright. npm run export:pptx Export the deck to PowerPoint format. Sharing npm run tunnel Start a Cloudflare Tunnel to share the dev server publicly. This requires cloudflared to be installed. The command starts the dev server and creates a public tunnel URL. Utilities npm run new Create a new slide file: This creates slides/slide-04.mdx with default frontmatter. npm run reorder Renumber slide files to maintain sequential ordering: All DexCode commands are standard npm scripts. You can inspect and modify them in package.json.

title: Configuration
description: Complete reference for deck.config.ts and slide frontmatter options.
content: deck.config.ts The deck configuration file defines project-level settings. Configuration Options Slide Frontmatter Each MDX slide file can include frontmatter to override defaults: Frontmatter Fields You can also configure theme settings by asking your AI agent: "Change the primary color to blue and use a light background."

title: Keyboard Shortcuts
description: Quick reference for all keyboard shortcuts in DexCode's slide viewer and presenter mode.
content: Slide Navigation | Shortcut | Action | |---|---| | → or Space | Next slide | | ← | Previous slide | | Home | First slide | | End | Last slide | | 1-9 | Jump to slide number | Presentation | Shortcut | Action | |---|---| | P | Toggle presenter mode | | F | Toggle fullscreen | | Esc | Exit fullscreen or presenter mode | | B | Black screen (toggle) | Development | Shortcut | Action | |---|---| | Cmd/Ctrl + S | Save current file (triggers HMR) | | Cmd/Ctrl + K | Open search (in docs) | | Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + R | Force reload | Presenter Mode When in presenter mode, additional shortcuts are available: | Shortcut | Action | |---|---| | T | Toggle timer | | R | Reset timer | | N | Toggle speaker notes visibility | | + / - | Increase / decrease notes font size | Keyboard shortcuts work in both the main viewer and the audience window. In presenter mode, the presenter window has priority for input.

title: Export Options
description: Export DexCode slides to PDF, PPTX, or static HTML for offline sharing.
content: Export Formats DexCode supports multiple export formats for different use cases. PDF Export Generate a high-quality PDF using Playwright's headless browser: How It Works 1. Playwright launches a headless Chromium browser 2. Each slide is rendered at the configured resolution (default: 1920x1080) 3. Slides are captured as PDF pages in landscape orientation 4. The output is saved to exports/deck.pdf Configuration Customize PDF export in deck.config.ts: PDF export requires Playwright to be installed. Run npx playwright install chromium if you haven't set it up yet. PPTX Export Export to PowerPoint format: Each slide is rendered as a high-resolution image and inserted into a PPTX file. This preserves the exact visual appearance of your slides. PPTX export creates image-based slides. Interactive elements (animations, hover effects) are captured as static snapshots. Static HTML Build a self-contained static site: The output in the out/ directory can be deployed to any static hosting service (GitHub Pages, Vercel, Netlify, S3, etc.). Image Export Export individual slides as PNG images: This creates one PNG file per slide in exports/slides/: Use Cases - Embedding individual slides in documents or emails - Social media sharing - Thumbnail generation
